legacy_api.description: AdvancedMD primary API is a ppmdmsg dispatcher. POST XML or JSON body with '@action' field to the XMLRPC endpoint. The @class is typically 'api' except for auth actions which use 'login'.
legacy_api.auth_flow: POST login action -> receive redirect error with webserver URL -> resubmit same body to redirected URL -> receive security token in <usercontext> -> include usercontext in subsequent calls

rest_api.description: REST endpoints run on a different webserver built by replacing 'processrequest' with 'api' (PM) or 'ehr-api' (EHR) in the webserver URL

endpoints.title: XML Format
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="action" class="class" msgtime="current time" [additional attributes required by action]> <usercontext ... /> [additional child elements as required by the action] </ppmdmsg>

endpoints.title: JSON Format
endpoints.sample_json: { "ppmdmsg": {"@action": "action","@class": "class","@msgtime": "current time ", (Additional objects, key value pairs, and arrays as required by the action) } }

endpoints.title: Requirements and Usage Restrictions
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="login" class="login" msgtime="7/23/2015 3:29:16 PM"

endpoints.notes: This token is good for 24 hours. You will need to have your interface login and refresh this token every 24 hours Version 2.2.7

endpoints.title: Get New and Modified Visits
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="getupdatedvisits" class="api" msgtime="7/24/2015 10:20:42 AM" datechanged="2015-03-24T8:25:28.730" visitid="1234" ignoreappttypes="1"> <visit date="VisitDate" duration="Duration" color="Color" /> <patient name="Name" ssn="SSN" changedat="ChangedAt" createdat="CreatedAt" /> <insurance carname="CarName" carcode="CarCode" carcity="CarCity" changedat="ChangedAt" createdat="CreatedAt"/> </ppmdmsg>
endpoints.notes: This call can now also bring back updated charges if the <charge> node is specified. The attribute, ignoreappttypes , when set to 1 allows to return visits that do not have an appointment type assigned, (previously this action could only search for visits with appointment type assigned). If attribute is not specified it's value defaults to 0 to preserve previous behavior and only return visits with appointment type assigned.

endpoints.title: Get a List of Fields Available to \"getupdatedvisits\"
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="getupdatedvisitstemplate" class="api" msgtime="7/24/2015 10:33:06 AM"> </ppmdmsg>
endpoints.notes: Attributes or key names can be named any valid attribute name. For example, if you are interested in the VisitDate field, you can call the attribute date, visit_date or visit_datetime. When you make the "getupdatedvisits" request, you indicate your attribute name as follows: attributename =" FieldName ", e.g. visit_date="VisitDate" or visit_datetime="VisitDate". Version 2.2.7
headers_outline: Get a List of Fields Available to "getupdatedvisits"

endpoints.title: Get Visits for a Specific Patient
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="getpatientvisits" class="api" msgtime="12/31/2015 4:55:49 AM" patientid="15985" appttype="" appttypeid="" apptstatusid="" referral="" referringproviderid="" referringprovider=""> <visit AppointmentTypeID="AppointmentTypeID" AppointmentType="AppointmentType" columnheading="ColumnHeading" ByReferringProviderFID="ByReferringProviderFID" ByRefProvLastName="ByRefProvLastName" ByRefProvFirstName="ByRefProvFirstName" ByRefProvMiddleName="ByRefProvMiddleName" apptstatus="ApptStatus" refreason="RefReason" duration="Duration" color="Color" /> <patient name="Name" ssn="SSN" changedat="ChangedAt" createdat="CreatedAt" /> <insurance carname="CarName" carcode="CarCode" changedat="ChangedAt" createdat="CreatedAt" /></ppmdmsg>;
endpoints.notes: Below the list of attributes applicable for getting the patient visits: patientid : (Required) This is the Patient ID appttype: (Optional) This is the Appointment Type name appttypeid : (Optional) This is the Appointment Type ID apptstatusid: (Optional) This is the Appointment Status ID referral: (Optional) This is the Referral Reason referringproviderid: (Optional) This is the Referring Provider ID referringprovider: (Optional) This is the Referring Provider name Version 2.2.7
headers_outline: Get Visits for a Specific Patient

endpoints.title: Get Visits for a Specific Date
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="getdatevisits" class="api" msgtime="7/24/2015 10:20:42 AM" visitdate="9/26/2015"> <visit columnheading="ColumnHeading" duration="Duration" color="Color" apptstatus="ApptStatus"/><patient name="Name" ssn="SSN" changedat="ChangedAt" createdat="CreatedAt" /> <insurance carname="CarName" carcode="CarCode" changedat="ChangedAt" createdat="CreatedAt"/> </ppmdmsg>
endpoints.notes: This method is recommended for use in retrieving a large amount of data. For example, using this method is preferred in order to retrieve a month, year, or several years of data. You should use this method to request all visits for a single day. You would need to submit the request repeatedly, once a day for the desired period of time you need. Once you have brought all of the visits down and synchronized your application, you can then begin to use the getupdatedvisits call to keep your database
headers_outline: Get Visits for a Specific Date

endpoints.title: Update a Patient
endpoints.sample_xml: <ppmdmsg action="updatepatient" class="api" msgtime="5/22/2015 2:48:08 PM" force="1"> <patientlist> <patient id="198655" o_employer="IBM, INC." employer="IBM, INC"> <address/> <contactinfo/> </patient> </patientlist> </ppmdmsg>
endpoints.notes: When updating a patient, only modified fields (old and new values) are submitted. The old value is placed in o_attributename and the new value is placed in attributename . In the above example, the o_employer contains the old value and the employer contains the new value. The old value is included so that the system can determine if another user has modified the value since the data was retrieved. If another user has modified, the following error message will be returned:
